# Live Music Coder — Full LLM Context

## Overview

Live Music Coder is a browser-based and Electron desktop live coding music IDE. Users write code to create music in real time using 4 audio engines. The app features a CodeMirror 6 code editor, a React Flow visual node graph, Canvas 2D audio visualizers (waveform, spectrum, timeline, pianoroll), and full i18n (DE/EN/ES). It ships as both a Netlify-hosted SPA and a native desktop app via Electron.

**Version:** 1.0.3
**Live:** https://live-music-coder.pro
**Repo:** https://github.com/arnoldwender/wm-prototyp-live-music-coder

## Metrics

- Test files: 24
- Tests: 154
- Components: 62 (17 atoms, 18 molecules, 27 organisms)
- Routes: 12
- Pages: 11 (Landing, Editor, Docs, Samples, Examples, Sessions, SessionPiece, Changelog, Blog, BlogPost, Legal)
- Examples: 215+
- Sessions: 51
- MIDI device profiles: 19
- Blog posts: 7
- Languages: 3 (DE, EN, ES)

## Architecture

### Code Editor = Source of Truth

The CodeMirror 6 editor is always authoritative. The React Flow node graph is a derived view, regenerated from code after every edit (500ms debounce). Graph interactions emit code mutations back to the editor.

### Engine Orchestrator

One shared AudioContext. Each engine adapter implements a common EngineAdapter interface. The Orchestrator class manages lazy engine initialization, code evaluation routing, and playback state.

### Audio Engines

| Engine    | Package            | Purpose                    |
|-----------|--------------------|----------------------------|
| Strudel   | @strudel/core      | Pattern-based live coding  |
| Tone.js   | tone               | High-level synths, effects |
| Web Audio | Native API         | Low-level audio nodes      |
| MIDI      | @strudel/midi + custom | Keyboard input (midikeys), CC knobs (midin), compose mode (play-to-code), MIDI learn (CC mapping), 19 device profiles, output |

### Atomic Design Hierarchy

- Atoms (17): ActivityBarButton, Badge, Button, CanvasVisualizer, EngineNode, ErrorBoundary, FilterCurve, Icon, Knob, Logo, NotFound, TabButton, Toggle, Tooltip, VirtualKeyboard, WaveformBackground, WaveformIcon
- Molecules (18): AchievementToast, EngineSelector, ErrorBar, FeatureCard, FileTabs, FilterControl, FilterPill, HelpPanel, LanguageSwitcher, MarkdownRenderer, NowPlayingIndicator, OscillatorSelector, SampleDropZone, ShareDialog, SortSelect, ToolbarGroup, VisualizerPills, VisualizerToggle
- Organisms (27): ActivityBar, CodeEditor, ConsolePanel, DetailPanel, ExampleGallery, FeatureGrid, GistDialog, HeroSection, MidiPanel, NodeGraph, PatternTimeline, PianorollVisualizer, PitchwheelVisualizer, PunchcardVisualizer, SettingsPanel, SidePanel, SiteNav, SpectrumVisualizer, SpiralVisualizer, StatusBar, StrudelEditor, SynthPanel, TemplateSelector, TransportBar, TutorialOverlay, VisualizerDashboard, WaveformVisualizer

Hierarchy rules: atoms import nothing, molecules import only atoms, organisms import molecules and atoms, layouts compose organisms, pages use layouts.

### MIDI Keyboard Input

Custom `midikeys()` implementation in `src/lib/midi/strudel-keys.ts` bypasses @strudel/midi's broken `getIsStarted()` check (caused by Vite's double @strudel/core module instance). Uses raw Web MIDI API + own AudioContext + OscillatorNode for zero-dependency sound. `midin()` for CC knobs delegates to @strudel/midi (CC reading doesn't have the isStarted bug). MIDI quick-action menu in the editor toolbar auto-loads test code when a controller is connected.

**Compose Mode** (`src/lib/midi/compose-mode.ts`): Play notes on a connected MIDI keyboard and have them convert to Strudel mini-notation in real time inside the editor. Supports quantization and auto-insert.

**MIDI Learn** (`src/lib/midi/midi-learn.ts`): Map any MIDI CC knob or fader to app parameters with a visual learn workflow. Connect hardware to software controls without editing code.

**19 Device Profiles** (`src/data/midi-devices.ts`): Factory CC mappings for MPK mini, Launchkey, Arturia KeyStep/MiniLab, Novation, Korg nanoKONTROL/minilogue, M-Audio, and more. Auto-detected by device name on connection.

**CRITICAL**: Never use `input.onmidimessage =` — use `addEventListener('midimessage', ...)`. The exclusive `onmidimessage` property steals events from @strudel/midi's webmidi library.

### Synth UI (Phase 1+2)

The SynthPanel organism (`src/components/organisms/SynthPanel.tsx`) appears when a MIDI device is connected. It composes:

- **VirtualKeyboard** (atom) — 2-octave on-screen piano. QWERTY mapping (a/w/s/e/d…). Velocity derived from Y-position of pointer on each key. Calls `window.__lmcPlayNote(note, velocity)`.
- **OscillatorSelector** (molecule) — 4 radio buttons (sine / sawtooth / square / triangle) that call `window.__lmcSetOscillator(type)`.
- **FilterControl** (molecule) — two Knob atoms (cutoff + resonance) + 4 type buttons (LP/HP/BP/Notch) + FilterCurve atom. Calls `window.__lmcSetFilter(type, cutoff, resonance)`.
- **Knob** (atom) — SVG arc knob. 270° travel. Pointer-drag for coarse, Shift+drag for fine (0.1× sensitivity). Double-click resets to default.
- **FilterCurve** (atom) — Canvas element rendering biquad frequency response 20Hz–20kHz on a log scale. Colors read from CSS variables via `getComputedStyle`. Redraws on every type/cutoff/resonance change.
- **WaveformIcon** (atom) — SVG path icons for the 4 waveform types.

**Audio path:** `strudel-keys.ts` exports `playOscillatorNote`, `setLmcOscillatorType`, and `setLmcFilter`. All three are exposed on `window` as `__lmcPlayNote`, `__lmcSetOscillator`, `__lmcSetFilter`. Physical MIDI keyboard and VirtualKeyboard share the identical code path. The filter stage is a `BiquadFilterNode` inserted per-note between oscillator and gain — created fresh each note so knob changes take effect immediately.

**Zustand state:** `synthOscillator`, `synthFilterType`, `synthFilterCutoff`, `synthFilterResonance` in `src/lib/store.ts`. `StrudelEditor` subscribes and calls `window.__lmcSetFilter` on changes.

### Piano Roll Visualizer

`PianorollVisualizer` (`src/components/organisms/PianorollVisualizer.tsx`) wraps `drawPianoroll()` (`src/lib/visualizers/pianoroll.ts`) which is called every RAF frame via `CanvasVisualizer`.

**React state owned by PianorollVisualizer:**
- `zoomX`, `zoomY` — time and pitch zoom (0.25–6×)
- `timeOffset` — cycles shifted from live position (positive = past)
- `velocityOverrides: Map<string, number>` — key `"note:start"`, value 0–1
- `pitchOverrides: Map<string, number>` — key `"note:start"`, value MIDI 0–127 (visual-only, does not mutate code)

**eventSinkRef** — mutable object populated each RAF frame by `drawPianoroll` with layout data for hit-testing without React re-renders: `{ events, timeStart, timeEnd, drawW, keysWidth, minNote, maxNote, noteHeight, yOffset }`. `noteHeight` and `yOffset` are physical canvas pixels — divide by `window.devicePixelRatio` to get CSS pixels for mouse event coordinates.

**HiDPI rule:** All hit-testing uses `canvas.getBoundingClientRect()` (CSS pixels), never `canvas.height` (physical pixels, 2× on Retina). The velocity lane threshold (`rect.height - 18`) and note Y position both use CSS coordinates.

**Interactions:**
- Click note bar → audition via `window.__lmcPlayNote?.(note)`
- Drag note bar vertically → pitch shifts by semitone, floating label shows `"C4 → D4"`, DPR-corrected: `semitones = Math.round(-dy / (noteHeight / dpr))`
- Drag velocity bar vertically → adjusts velocity override
- Drag background horizontally → pans timeline
- Cursor: `grab` / `ns-resize` (during note/velocity drag) / `grabbing` (pan)
- Reset button (↺) clears both override maps

**Canvas colors:** `VIZ_COLORS` in `src/lib/visualizers/colors.ts` — all Canvas 2D palette values live here (cannot use CSS variables in Canvas context).

### Persistence

- IndexedDB autosave via idb library
- URL sharing via lz-string compression (#code=...)
- GitHub Gist save/load via @octokit/rest with PAT
- Audio recording via MediaRecorder → WebM
- Native file save/open via Electron IPC (desktop only)

## Electron Desktop App

### Runtime

Electron 41.2.0 (Node v22 bundled), built with electron-vite 5 and electron-builder 26. macOS builds are signed with Developer ID Application and notarized via Apple's native `@electron/notarize` (not the deprecated `electron-builder-notarize` afterSign hook). Auto-updater metadata (`latest-mac.yml`, `-mac.zip` + blockmaps, `.dmg` + blockmaps) is published to GitHub Releases.

### Directory Structure

```text
electron/
├── main.ts          # Main process: BrowserWindow creation, app lifecycle
├── preload.ts       # contextBridge: exposes electronAPI to renderer
├── menu.ts          # Native application menu (File, Edit, View, Help)
├── tray.ts          # System tray icon and playback controls
├── store.ts         # electron-store for persistent user settings
├── updater.ts       # electron-updater for auto-updates
├── wav-encoder.ts   # PCM-to-WAV encoder for native file export
└── ipc/
    ├── app.ts       # App info, notifications, quit, update checks
    ├── audio.ts     # WAV file export via native save dialog
    ├── file.ts      # Project save/open/recent files
    └── window.ts    # Panel pop-out, fullscreen toggle
```

### IPC Channels

| Channel                     | Direction       | Purpose                           |
|-----------------------------|-----------------|-----------------------------------|
| app:info                    | invoke → result | Get app name, version, platform   |
| app:notify                  | send            | Show native OS notification       |
| app:quit                    | send            | Quit the application              |
| app:check-update            | send            | Trigger auto-update check         |
| file:save                   | invoke → result | Save project JSON (Save As dialog)|
| file:save-path              | invoke → result | Save to specific file path        |
| file:open                   | invoke → result | Open project file (Open dialog)   |
| file:recent                 | invoke → result | Get recent files list              |
| file:reveal                 | send            | Reveal file in Finder/Explorer    |
| audio:export-wav            | invoke → result | Export audio buffer as WAV file   |
| window:popout               | send            | Pop out a panel to new window     |
| window:fullscreen           | send            | Toggle fullscreen mode            |
| window:fullscreen-changed   | on (listener)   | Fullscreen state changed          |
| window:popout-closed        | on (listener)   | Pop-out window was closed         |
| menu:action                 | on (listener)   | Native menu action dispatched     |

### Build Configuration

electron.vite.config.ts defines three build targets:
- **main:** electron/main.ts → out/main/main.cjs (CJS — allows native `__dirname` usage post-bundle)
- **preload:** electron/preload.ts → out/preload/preload.cjs (CJS)
- **renderer:** index.html + src/ → dist/ with `base: './'` (standard Vite build with React + Tailwind; relative base is required for file:// loading in packaged app)

### Build Targets

- macOS: .dmg (universal + arm64), via `npm run electron:build:mac`
- Windows: .exe installer, via `npm run electron:build:win`
- Linux: .AppImage, via `npm run electron:build:linux`

## Routes (12)

| Route             | Page          | Description                                |
|-------------------|---------------|--------------------------------------------|
| /                 | Landing       | Hero, features, examples (also Electron splash) |
| /editor           | Editor        | Main IDE: code, graph, visualizers         |
| /docs             | Docs          | Documentation hub                          |
| /docs/:sectionId  | Docs          | Individual doc section                     |
| /samples          | Samples       | Audio sample library browser               |
| /examples         | Examples      | Curated code examples per engine           |
| /sessions         | Sessions      | Curated AI-composed pieces — listing       |
| /sessions/:slug   | SessionPiece  | Individual piece (code, movements, notes)  |
| /changelog        | Changelog     | Project changelog                          |
| /blog             | Blog          | Developer blog — listing                   |
| /blog/:slug       | BlogPost      | Individual blog post                       |
| /legal            | Legal         | Impressum, Datenschutz, Lizenzen           |

## License Structure

The project uses a dual-license model:

- **Combined application (AGPL-3.0-or-later):** The @strudel/* packages are AGPL-licensed. Because they are runtime dependencies, the combined application inherits the AGPL-3.0-or-later license. This means the full source must be available when the app is served over a network.
- **Original components (MIT):** Files authored by Arnold Wender that do not depend on AGPL code are marked with `SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT` and can be reused under MIT terms.
- **Details:** See LICENSING.md in the repository root.

Copyright (c) 2026 Arnold Wender / Wender Media

## Key Constraints

- Dark mode only (music production aesthetic)
- NEVER hardcode colors/spacing — use CSS custom property design tokens
- Canvas 2D cannot use CSS variables — use VIZ_COLORS from src/lib/visualizers/colors.ts
- This is Vite + React SPA — NOT Next.js, no server components, no "use client"
- Colocated tests: foo.test.ts next to foo.ts
- Barrel exports: index.ts per component directory
- Node v22 for Netlify builds; Node v22 bundled with Electron 41
- Router: conditional (HashRouter in Electron, BrowserRouter on web) — `/` renders the Landing hero on both targets; the hero "Start Coding" CTA links to /editor
- Editor handoff (Examples, Samples, Sessions → /editor) uses `navigate('/editor', { state: { share: { code, bpm, engine } } })` instead of a URL hash — HashRouter in Electron reserves the URL fragment for routing, so `#code=xxx` gets clobbered. Editor reads `location.state.share` first, then falls back to `readShareFromUrl()` for backwards-compat with external share links that arrive via the URL on the web
- CSP with `'unsafe-eval'` is required for Strudel pattern evaluation (set via session.webRequest.onHeadersReceived in electron/main.ts)
- Renderer Helper entitlements MUST include `com.apple.security.cs.allow-jit` in build/entitlements.mac.inherit.plist — without it V8 SIGTRAPs during Isolate initialization (v1.0.1 black-screen regression)
- Electron macOS window uses `titleBarStyle: 'hiddenInset'`. A custom <TitleBar /> drag strip is rendered when `isElectronMac` (src/lib/platform.ts), reserving 28px at the top of the window with `-webkit-app-region: drag` and matching body padding via the `.electron-mac` class set on <html> from main.tsx.

## Sessions category

`/sessions` is a curated collection of live-coding pieces authored inside a single conversation with an AI model. 51 pieces across 15 genres: Ambient, Blues, Breakbeat, Deep Work, Drum & Bass, Dub, Dub Techno, Electronic, Flamenco, Jazz, Lo-Fi, Narrative, Retro, Techno, Trance. Each piece has a stable slug (e.g. `/sessions/jede-session-neu-geboren`) and its own detail route because the long-form narrative context (composer notes, movements, key signatures) doesn't fit the flat Examples grid. Entries are defined in `src/data/sessions-library.ts` via the `SessionEntry` schema (slug, title, subtitle, author with `kind: 'ai' | 'human'` and optional model, date, bpm, durationSec, short description, composer notes, movements, engine, code). The `SessionCategory` type union includes all 15 genre values; `SESSION_CATEGORIES` is the ordered array for filter UI. The primary action on a piece is "Open in Editor", which passes the code via react-router `location.state` to `/editor` — the same flow Examples and Samples use. There is no external strudel.cc link. Sessions pages reuse the shared site nav and design tokens so the category feels like a sibling of /samples and /examples rather than a standalone sub-site. Content (the piece itself, composer notes) is authored in German; chrome labels are translated under the `sessions.*` i18n namespace.
